name: Black Bean
recipeIngredient: 14 ounces stewed tomatoes, Mexican Style, drained and strained
10 ounces whole kernel corn, frozen or 8 ounces canned
1/2 cup chopped red bell pepper (or less)
1 chicken breast, grilled and cut into strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Select small oval black beans. Drain canned beans in a sieve and rinse thoroughly under cold water.
2. Try canned stewed tomatoes with Jalapeno peppers, chili powder, cumin, and pepper (Del Monte has one; S&W). Drain, reserve the liquid; chop the tomatoes (thumbnail size). Avoid seeds and watery pulp.
3. Select a light salad oil: olive, safflower, corn, canola, blend, etc.
4. Combine ingredients for "optionals" before adding them to the mixture. Taste and add salt, pepper, a few more chilies, cinnamon, and/or oregano as desired. If too dry, add some tomato liquid. Store in the refrigerator until well chilled, at least 4 hours.
5. Serve as a salad on shredded iceberg or mixed leaf lettuce. It also makes a great filling for a QUESADILLA with a little shredded Monterey Jack cheese! (Heat skillet to medium high; spray one side of a flour tortilla with vegetable oil; place oiled side down in the skillet; spoon on some filling. Top with cheese. Cover with another tortilla. After about 2 minutes, spray the top tortilla and turn it; brown about 1 minute. Serve with sauce casera.)
